summary: DOP attributes the rise in the adult probation-violation rate through Fiscal 2024 largely to an increase in rearrests among people on probation, which drives up the overall count of violations. The subsequent decline to 1.5% in Fiscal 2025 is credited by the agency to enhanced supervision efforts aimed at helping clients stay in compliance with the terms of probation.

sources.label: Department of Probation, Fiscal 2024 Mayor's Management Report
Department of Probation, Fiscal 2025 Mayor's Management Report

oneLiner: increasing overall, though easing recently with enhanced supervision efforts
